text: **Sue:** {Welcome|ようこそ/歓迎|/ˈwelkəm/|Welcome to our party.} to class, Anna.
**Sue:** I can't wait to see your {trash|ごみ/廃棄物|/træʃ/|Take out the trash, please.}
**Sue:** Oh, okay.
**Sue:** Alright.
**Anna:** It's an it's a {net|網/ネット|/net/|Fishing net catches fish.} with a oh that's got a {hole|穴|/hoʊl/|There's a hole in my sock.} in it.
**Anna:** Oh tell me about that
**Sue:** This net said to me, Anna, I used to {catch|捕まえる/捕獲する|/kætʃ/|Catch the ball quickly.} stuff.
**Anna:** I don't anymore.
**Sue:** So
**Sue:** Use me.
**Sue:** Anna.
**Anna:** That's what it said.
**Anna:** It said use me, Anna.
**Sue:** Okay.
**Sue:** So
**Anna:** It's a {plastic|プラスチックの|/ˈplæstɪk/|Plastic bags are harmful.} helmet.
**Anna:** This plastic helmet said Anna.
**Anna:** Hey, Anna, find me a head.
**Sue:** Okay.
**Sue:** Um
**Sue:** And a {broken|壊れた/こわれた|/ˈbroʊkən/|My broken toy doesn't work.} toy.
**Sue:** This broken toy, this broken toy said to me, it said Anna.
**Anna:** Help me find fun.
**Anna:** Help me, Anna, is what it said.
**Anna:** Anna, this stuff is not saying anything to you or me or anyone.
**Anna:** It's what we like to call in the business
**Anna:** {Garbage|ごみ/廃棄物|/ˈɡɑːrbɪdʒ/|Take out the garbage today.}
**Anna:** But you said to bring in trash.
**Sue:** Sometimes {trash|ごみ/廃棄物|/træʃ/|Take out the trash, please.} is {treasure|宝/貴重なもの|/ˈtreʒər/|This old book is a treasure.}
**Sue:** Sometimes it's just trash.
**Sue:** Anna, don't worry.
**Sue:** Next week I could teach you {decoupage|装飾技法/切り絵細工|/ˈdekəpɑːʒ/|Decoupage is an art form}.
**Anna:** Decoupage.
**Anna:** That's fun to say.
**Sue:** All you need to bring is a clean {shoe box|靴箱|/ʃuː bɒks/|Put your shoes in the box}.
**Sue:** A box that held shoes.
**Anna:** I got it.
**Anna:** See you next week.
**Anna:** Uh-oh, it looks like Anna doesn't know the difference between {treasure|宝物/財宝|/ˈtreʒər/|This treasure is valuable.} and trash.
**Anna:** She thinks those things are {telling|伝える/告げる|/ˈtelɪŋ/|He is telling a story.} her something.
**Anna:** The words {tell|伝える/言う|/tel/|Tell me the truth.} and say have similar meanings.
**Anna:** But we use them in different ways.
**Anna:** Tell means to inform or instruct someone with words
**Anna:** and is almost always followed by an indirect object.
**Anna:** For example, Sue says, Tell me about that.
**Anna:** The word me is the indirect object and tells us who is being told.
**Anna:** Say means to express something with words and focuses more on the words used
**Anna:** For example, Anna says.
**Anna:** That's what it said.
**Anna:** It said, use me, Anna.
**Anna:** Listen for when Anna and Sue use tell and say.
**Sue:** Anna, welcome to decoupage class.
**Anna:** Decoupage is just {gluing|接着する/貼り付ける|/ɡluːɪŋ/|Gluing paper to wood.} pretty pictures onto stuff.
**Anna:** Hold up.
**Sue:** I've gotta talk to this person, I'll be right back.
**Sue:** Don't start without me.
**Anna:** Got it
**Sue:** Anna, what happened?
**Anna:** I told you to wait for me.
**Sue:** Actually, you told me not to start without you.
**Anna:** Which I didn't.
**Anna:** Your {glue|接着剤/のり|/ɡluː/|I need glue for my craft.} is really {sticky|べたべた|/ˈstɪki/|This tape is very sticky.}
**Sue:** Next week, let's try {lamp|ランプ/照明器具|/læmp/|This lamp lights up the room.} making.
**Sue:** There's there's no glue.
**Sue:** Here's a {flyer|チラシ/パンフレット|/ˈflaɪər/|I got a flyer about the event.}
**Anna:** Thanks.
**Anna:** See you next week.
**Sue:** Anna, you did really well this week.
**Sue:** I think {lamp|ランプ/照明器具|/læmp/|This lamp is beautiful.} making might be your thing.
**Anna:** Thanks, Sue.
**Anna:** I did everything you told me to do.
**Anna:** Remember, I said to read the {instructions|説明書/手順|/ɪnˈstrʌkʃənz/|Read the instructions carefully.} really carefully.
**Anna:** Where are those instructions?
**Anna:** Let's just {plug|差し込む/接続する|/plʌɡ/|Plug in the charger.} it in.
**Anna:** So what class will you be teaching next week?
**Anna:** Too bad Anna didn't follow {instructions|説明書/手順|/ɪnˈstrʌkʃənz/|Always read the instructions.}
**Anna:** Visit our website for more on Tell and Say.
